fields.System.Title: [Data] Spike: Admin Portal (AP) Drug API Coverage & Clinical Data Model Validation for NewUM R1 (ICD-10, Drugs, Regimens)
fields.System.Description:
Objectives:


Confirm that Admin Portal (Drug API) contains and exposes all required clinical data for:
  1. [V1.R1.] Diagnosis Selection & Details for Case
  2. [V1.R1.] Main Regimen Selection
  3. [V1.R1.] Allow Custom Regimens
  4. [V1.R1.] Select Ancillary Drugs
  5. [V1.R1.] Capture Basic Dosing Details
  6. [V1.R1.] Apply Payer Drug Configurations
  7. [V1.R1.] Apply Payer Non-Preferred Drug Rules
fields.Microsoft.VSTS.Common.AcceptanceCriteria:
The investigation should cover:

  1. ICD 10 Data:
    1. Availability of ICD-10 codes and descriptions
    2. Diagnosis Group mapping
    3. Cancer stage configuration 
    4. Treatment type applicability (Chemo / GMT / XRT)
    5. Payer-specific ICD logic (common vs payer-extended codes)
  2. Master Drug + PDL (Payer Drug Library) Overlay Model
    1. Drug identifiers (NDC / GPI / HCPCS etc.):
      1. Brand/Generic Drug Name
      2. Alternative Drugs
      3. Ancillary Drugs
    2. EADL flags (if applicable)
    3. Dosing Metadata
      1. Default dosing parameters
      2. Calculation logic (BSA, flat dose, etc.)
  3. Regimen Library
    1. Regimen → Diagnosis Group linkage
    2. Regimen drug composition
Expected Deliverables
  1. Confirm whether AP can serve as the single source of truth for clinical + payer configuration
  2. Identify gaps in API coverage or data modeling
  3. Clarify rule execution ownership (AP vs NewUM)
  4. Recommend architecture approach (direct API vs cache/hybrid)
  5. Highlight R1 risks and ERD impacts

fields.System.Title: [FE] Select Cancer Stage for Diagnosis
fields.System.Description: As a CRS, Provider Staff, or Health Plan User, I want to select a cancer stage relevant to the chosen diagnosis, So that reviewers have appropriate clinical context during evaluation.
fields.Microsoft.VSTS.Common.AcceptanceCriteria:
1. The system should require an ICD-10 diagnosis to be selected before a Cancer Stage value can be entered:
    1. Data Dictionary
  2. The system should limit Cancer Stage options to values relevant to the selected diagnosis (Diagnosis Group).
  3. The system should require Cancer Stage selection before the user can proceed to the next workflow step.
  4. The system should allow selection of exactly one Cancer Stage value.
  5. The system should save the selected Cancer Stage on the case.
  6. If the user changes the selected ICD-10 diagnosis, the system should clear the previously selected Cancer Stage.
  7. After ICD-10 change, the system should require Cancer Stage to be selected again before proceeding.

fields.System.Title: [BE] SPIKE: Investigate Payer applicability
fields.System.Description: https://oncologyanalytics.visualstudio.com/newUM/_workitems/edit/183544
fields.Microsoft.VSTS.Common.AcceptanceCriteria:
Scope

  1. Understand how payer applicability for ICD-10 diagnoses is stored and exposed from the Admin Portal data model.
  2. Confirm how “all payer options selected” is represented in source data and how it should be interpreted in NewUM.
  3. Determine which health plan identifier from member eligibility should be used for filtering.

fields.System.Title: [FE] Select Tx Type (Chemo vs GMT vs XRT)
fields.System.Description: As CRS team member, I want to select the assignment type for the case, so that the system understands what type of treatment is being requested before protocol details are entered.
fields.Microsoft.VSTS.Common.AcceptanceCriteria:
1. After Diagnosis selection is successfully completed in accordance with [V1.R1.] Diagnosis Selection & Details for Case , the system should allow the user to proceed to Treatment Type selection.

  2. The system should display the following Treatment Type options:

    1. Chemotherapy (Chemo):
      1. should be enabled and selectable for V1.R1.
    2. XRT (Radiation):
      1. should be disabled for V1.R1.
    3. GMT (Test):
      1. should be disabled for V1.R1.
  3. Each selectable option should correspond to a distinct Treatment Type value stored at the case level.
  4. The system should require selection of a Treatment Type before allowing navigation to treatment-specific details.
  5. The system should prevent the user from proceeding to any treatment workflow if no Treatment Type is selected.

  6. Upon selection of a Treatment Type, the system should:

    1. Persist the selected Treatment Type in the case context.
    2. Initialize the corresponding treatment workflow.
    3. Route the user to the appropriate treatment entry screen.
  7. The system should persist the selected Treatment Type when the case is saved in Draft status.
  8. The system should restore the previously selected Treatment Type when a Draft case is reopened.
Considerations:
  • Each Treatment Type should map to a distinct workflow configuration and associated data model.
  • Routing logic should be enforced server-side to prevent invalid state transitions.
  • Treatment Type should be stored as a controlled enumeration to support future expansion (e.g., Radiation, Test).

fields.System.Title: [BE] Create Configuration Repository
fields.System.Description:
See https://oncologyanalytics.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/NewUM1/pages/5140742173/DRAFT+Configuration+Guide#Configurations for additional context

  • We need to be able to save various configurations that will handle how NewUM behaves for particular payers or use-cases
    • This configuration repository will not be consumed by any other system
  • Access to configurations should be restricted, with only dedicated OH configuration administrator being able to make updates
  • While building dedicated user friendly UI to manage rules and configurations is OOS for phase 1 of the project, when designing this solution attention should be given that UI features can be implemented in the future without need for significant refactoring.
    • NewUM configurations and rules need to be readable and manageable by a non-engineer, i.e. changes in configuration and updating of the rules should not require code changes and don’t rely on SDLC.
  • NewUM configuration repository should support:
    • General system configurations
      • Business rules related settings defined on OH level that are valid for all payers
      • This is the highest level configuration, and as a rule, any lower level configuration overrides it
    • Payer specific configuration and behavior feature flags
      • Business rules related settings

      • These can be drilled down further to LoB level->Product line level->Employer group level

    • Outcomes
      • While possible determinations will remain as-is, it’s possible that for some payers we’ll need some flexibility to configure nuances around them such as messaging
    • Workload Queue Configuration

      • Workload queues will be used as “targets” for case assignment. Case is assigned to queue vs individual, as each queue can contain multiple OH employees. OH configuration admin needs to be able to manage assignment queues

        • Create new assignment queue

        • Update existing assignment queue

        • Retire existing assignment queue

          • This is a form of soft-delete: queue still exists in database for auditing and reporting purposes, but new cases cannot be assigned to it

        • Add OH personnel accounts to queue

          • Account can be added to multiple queues

        • Remove OH personnel accounts from queue

          • TBD

    • UI Config - there is a specific ticket for this #184320
  • Configurations are hierarchical and hierarchy can be configured 
    • Users need to be able to configure hierarchy of level (parent-child relationships)
      • This is handled explicitly by #184321
    • Lowest level configuration always takes precedence
fields.Microsoft.VSTS.Common.AcceptanceCriteria:
1. Create new or update existing DB structure(s)
    1. Must support following data fields:

      Column Name

      Description

      Notes

      ID

      UUID

       

      Type

      Type configuration applies to

       Allowed values from https://oncologyanalytics.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/NewUM1/pages/5140742173/DRAFT+Configuration+Guide#Configuration-Type 

      Grain Level

      Grain Level which configuration applies

       Allowed values from https://oncologyanalytics.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/NewUM1/pages/5140742173/DRAFT+Configuration+Guide#Grain-Level

      Level Values

      Values to read level for 

       e.g. if level is payer, this field would contain payer(s) name configuration applies for, if level is employer group, this column would contain employer group names to which configuration applies for

      Configuration Name

      Unique name of configuration record

       

      Display Name

      Configuration name to be displayed to end users

       Potentially on FE interfaces, audit logs, rule execution details etc.

      Description

      Short description of configuration record

       

      Value

      Value of configuration record. This is the value that the programmable logic will test against.

      Can be boolean, text, int, varchar, range

       e.g. for configuration name ="states where we do business", value field would contain list of states ,

      for configuration name "NP assignment queue" this fields will contain list of OH NPs case can be assigned to

      Status

      Status of the configuration record

       allowed values from https://oncologyanalytics.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/NewUM1/pages/5127569409/Rules+and+configurations+functional+specification#Rule-and-Configuration-Lifecycle 

      Valid From

      Date record is valid from

       

      Valid To

      Date record is valid to

       

    2. Feel free to add other entities necessary for technical implementation (e.g enumerators, lookup tables etc.)
  2. Create APIs to enable FE to consume and manage data
    1. Read endpoint available to everyone
    2. Create & Update endpoints available only to users in OH Admin role (role name TBC)
    3. No delete endpoint
  3. Validations:
    1. Only 1 record with combination of values Type+Grain Level+Grain Value+Configuration Name can be active at any given time (check ValidTo and ValidFrom dates and status)
    2. Mandatory: ID, Type, Configuration Name, Value, Status, Valid From
    3. If Grain Level is not empty, than  Level Value is mandatory
  4. All changes have to be audited according to NFR, special ticket for it #184180

fields.System.Title: [BE] Create Rules Repository
fields.System.Description:
See https://oncologyanalytics.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/NewUM1/pages/5140742173/DRAFT+Configuration+Guide#Rules for additional context

  • We need to be able to save various rules that will handle how NewUM behaves for particular payers or use-cases
    • This rule configuration repository will not be consumed by any other system
  • Access to rule configurations should be restricted, with only dedicated OH configuration administrator being able to make updates
  • While building dedicated user friendly UI to manage rules and configurations is OOS for phase 1 of the project, when designing this solution attention should be given that UI features can be implemented in the future without need for significant refactoring.
    • NewUM configurations and rules need to be readable and manageable by a non-engineer, i.e. changes in configuration and updating of the rules should not require code changes and don’t rely on SDLC.
  • NewUM rules configuration repository should support:
    • Rule execution setup
      • Defining workflow, steps and events that rule needs to trigger on
        • Workflow configuration is OOS of this ticket, there is an explicit ticket for that #184319
    • Defining rule tiers
      • 3 tiers (1,2,3)
    • Payer specific configuration and behavior feature flags
      • Business rules related settings

      • These can be drilled down further to LoB level->Product line level->Employer group level

    • Defining conditions rule is checking, result of the check is always SUCCESS or FAIL
      • Multiple conditions can be nested
    • Defining actions that need to be taken on each result
      • Multiple actions can be specified
    • Rule configurations are hierarchical (grain levels)

      • Even tho some configuration level can be shared (e.g. LoBs), each configuration/rule should be tied to specific payer org they apply to

    • Users need to be able to configure hierarchy of grain levels (parent-child relationships) on payer level
      • This is OOS of this ticket and handled explicitly by #184321
      • Lowest grain level configuration always takes precedence
    • Effective Dating
      • Valid from and Valid to metadata parameters that rules engine will consume in order to determine should rule be triggered
fields.Microsoft.VSTS.Common.AcceptanceCriteria:
1. Create new or update existing DB structure(s)
    1. Must support following data fields:

    2. Feel free to add other entities necessary for technical implementation (e.g enumerators, lookup tables etc.)
  2. Create APIs to enable FE to consume and manage data
    1. Read endpoint available to everyone with active NewUM access
    2. Create & Update endpoints available only to users in OH Admin role (role name TBC)
    3. No delete endpoint
  3. Validations:

    2. Mandatory: ID, Workflow, Rule Name, Tier, Step, Event, Conditions, Status, ValidFrom

  4. All changes have to be audited according to NFR, special ticket for that #184183

fields.System.Title: [FE] Search Existing Regimens by Drug Composition (Drug Name, Brand/Generic, or HCPCS)
fields.System.Description: As a CRS, or CRR, I want to search for main regimens using drug name(s) (brand or generic) or HCPCS codes , so that I can locate regimens based on their drug composition.
fields.Microsoft.VSTS.Common.AcceptanceCriteria:
1. The system should allow the user to enter a search regimen by:
    1. HCPCS code
    2. Drug Brand Name
    3. Drug Generic Name
  2. When a search value is entered, the system should match it against drug records in the drug library using brand name, generic name, HCPCS code, and their associated relationships
  3. A regimen should be returned if it contains at least one drug that corresponds to the matched drug record from the drug library:
    1. If a brand name is entered, the system should return regimens that contain either that brand or its linked generic version.
    2. If a generic name is entered, the system should return regimens that contain either that generic or its linked brand version.
    3. If an HCPCS code is entered, the system should return regimens that contain the drug tied to that HCPCS code.
  4. Search should support partial match (fuzzy/typeahead behavior):
    1. The system shall return diagnosis-filtered regimen results when the user enters at least 3 characters of a regimen-related search term, including partial text matches on HCPCS code or drug name.
    2. The system shall support limited typo tolerance for drug-name searches only and shall rank exact HCPCS code matches and exact drug-name matches above partial or approximate matches.
  5. Search results should return regimen-level entries only (not individual drugs):
    1. If multiple drugs within a regimen match the search criteria, the regimen shall appear only once in the result set.
  6. Search Results Display:
    1. The system shall use the descriptive text inside parentheses from the Admin Portal regimen label as the preferred regimen display name
    2. If the descriptive parenthetical text cannot be extracted reliably, the system shall display the full Admin Portal regimen label as fallback and shall not show a truncated, partially parsed, or reconstructed title.
    3. the system should not show the Diagnosis Group in the result row.
    4. the system should not show protocol-status values (for example, Auto, Non-Auto, S2
      1. Example:
        1. Source Admin Portal label : 
          carboplatin, paclitaxel, pembrolizumab (Pembrolizumab + Weekly PACLitaxel + Weekly CARBOplatin (Neoadjuvant Course 1 for Early Stage TNBC))
        2. Expected display: 
          Pembrolizumab + Weekly PACLitaxel + Weekly CARBOplatin (Neoadjuvant Course 1 for Early Stage TNBC)
        3. Valid fallback if extraction is not reliable: 
          carboplatin, paclitaxel, pembrolizumab (Pembrolizumab + Weekly PACLitaxel + Weekly CARBOplatin (Neoadjuvant Course 1 for Early Stage TNBC))
  7. Search results should be limited to regimens associated with the Diagnosis Group already selected on the case in accordance with Retrieve Diagnosis-Scoped Regimens from Regimen Library
  8. Ancillary drugs (drug type=ancillary) should not appear as standalone regimen search results.
  9. If no regimens match the search criteria within the diagnosis-scoped set, the result should be empty.
Technical considerations:
  • Paging and incremental loading should be supported.
  • Ancillary-only drugs must not appear as standalone results.

fields.System.Title: [FE] Allow Switching Between Equivalent Drugs Based on Shared RxCUI
fields.System.Description: As a CRS, or CRR, I want to switch between equivalent drugs within a regimen when appropriate so that I can select the correct billable product without changing the clinical intent.
fields.Microsoft.VSTS.Common.AcceptanceCriteria:
1. User should be able to switch between equivalent drugs based on shared RxCUI during Chemo entry.
  2. For each case-level drug the system should determine whether eligible alternative drugs exist:
    1. the system should identify drugs sharing the same ingredient RxCUI.
    2. the system should consistently apply the same alternative-identification logic to Existing regimen drugs, Custom regimen drugs, Ancillary drugs
  3. User should be able to select the alternative drug in the dosing table:
    1.  A switching control (HCPCS dropdown) should be visible/enabled only when alternative drugs exist
    2. The generic drug must be selected as the default option when alternatives exist.
  4. Alternatives must be grouped and ordered as follows:
    1. Generic drugs where Route = IV, ordered alphabetically by Drug Name if multiple exist
    2. Generic drugs where Route = Other, (including PO/oral generics), ordered alphabetically by Drug Name if multiple exist
    3. Brand drugs where  Drug Qualifier = 505(b)(2), ordered alphabetically by Drug Name if multiple exist
    4. Drugs where Drug Qualifier = Biosimilar, ordered alphabetically by Drug Name if multiple exist
  5. When an alternative drug is selected for a case-level drug, the system should update the HCPCS code and drug description to match the selected drug.

  6. Each switch should be logged as an audit event with: 

    Previous selection, New selection, User, Timestamp
Assumptions:
  1. DrugAPI returns alternative drugs, including Route information to support sorting. Additional research is required for any other alternative-selection logic.

fields.System.Title: [FE] Create Custom Regimen on Case Entry
fields.System.Description: As a CRR/CRS team member, I want to create a Custom Chemotherapy Regimen during case entry so that I can proceed when no predefined regimen is available.
fields.Microsoft.VSTS.Common.AcceptanceCriteria:
1. The system should allow the user to create a Custom Chemotherapy Regimen only when no predefined regimens are returned for the drugs used in the regimen search:
    1. The system should enable Custom Regimen creation after a regimen search has been performed and no matching predefined regimens are identified.
  2. The user should be able to search for drugs in a manner consistent with the existing drug search behavior, using drug name (brand or generic) or HCPCS code
  3. The system should return matching drugs in Custom Regimen drug search results using the format [HCPCS code]  [Drug Name] ([Brand Name]) when a brand name is available.
    1. The system should exclude drugs flagged as Ancillary drugs from the search results
    2. The system should use the same display format for Medical and Pharmacy drugs, with no Pharmacy-specific variation in result presentation.
    3. The system should use the clinically recognizable drug name from the source record as the primary displayed drug name and shall append the brand name in parentheses as a qualifier.
      1. Example:
        1. Source Admin: Drug Name: 3-iodobenzylguanidine, Brand Name: Azedra [2053287], HCPCS: A9590
        2. Expected display: A9590  3-iodobenzylguanidine (Azedra)
    4. The system should start returning matching results only after the user enters at least 3 characters.
    5. The system should support partial-match search and limited typo tolerance for drug-name searches, while ranking exact matches above partial or approximate matches.
  4. The user should be able to select and add drugs for the Custom Regimen:
    1. The system should require that a Custom Regimen contain at least one drug before it can be saved.
  5. The system should add each selected drug to the case-level regimen record upon selection.
  6. For drugs added to a Custom Regimen, the system should allow switching between equivalent drugs based on shared RxCUI and should exclude drugs flagged as EADL from the eligible equivalent drug set when presenting switching options - in accordance with:
    1. Allow Switching Between Equivalent Drugs Based on Shared RxCUI
    2. Restrict Selection of EADL-Flagged Drugs in Regimen Drug Selection
  7. The system should not persist the Custom Regimen as a predefined regimen available for future selection in other chemo cases.

fields.System.Title: [FE] Validate Custom Regimen Against Existing Regimens
fields.System.Description: As a CRR/CRS team member, I want the system to validate a Custom Chemotherapy Regimen against predefined regimens so that I can reuse an existing standard regimen when the selected drug set already matches one.
fields.Microsoft.VSTS.Common.AcceptanceCriteria:
1. The system should evaluate the Custom Regimen drug set after the user has added drugs to the Custom Regimen.
  2. The system should compare the Custom Regimen drug set to predefined regimen drug sets:
    1. The system should limit the comparison to predefined regimens mapped to the Diagnosis Group selected for the case in accordance with Retrieve Diagnosis-Scoped Regimens from Regimen Library
  3. The system should determine a match only when the Custom Regimen drug set matches exactly to a predefined regimen drug set (set equality, not partial match).
    1. If no exact match is found, the system should allow the user to continue without additional prompts.
    2. If match is found, the system should notify the user and present the matching predefined regimen
  4. The user should be able to replace the Custom Regimen with a selected predefined regimen.
  5. The user should be able to keep the Custom Regimen and continue case entry.
  6. The system should record whether the user replaced the Custom Regimen or continued with it when an exact match is presented.

fields.System.Title: TECH DEBT
[BE] Capture Patient Height and Weight
fields.System.Description: As a CRS or CSM user, I want to enter and update patient height and weight so that dosing calculations can be performed accurately.
fields.Microsoft.VSTS.Common.AcceptanceCriteria:
1. The system should require the user to enter patient Height and Weight before allowing Main Regimen selection, Custom Regimen drug addition, or Ancillary Drug addition for Chemotherapy.
  2. The system should allow capture of the following case-level data in the UI:
    1. Preferred Measurement System
    2. Height
    3. Weight
  3. The system should provide the following Preferred Measurement System options:
    1. Standard (ft/in/lb) - displayed by default
    2. Standard (in/lb)
    3. Metric (cm/kg)
  4. The system should use the selected Preferred Measurement System to control the Height input format and Weight input unit as follows:
    1. Standard (ft/in/lb): Height captured as feet and inches; Weight captured in pounds
    2. Standard (in/lb): Height captured in inches; Weight captured in pounds
    3. Metric (cm/kg): Height captured in centimeters; Weight captured in kilograms
  5. The system should convert and store normalized values in calculation-ready units as follows:
    1. Height in centimeters
    2. Weight in kilograms
  6. The system should validate Height and Weight according to the rules defined in the Patient Height & Weight  and the selected Preferred Measurement System.
  7. The system should automatically calculate BSA in the background using normalized Height and Weight in accordance with the following formula: BSA = √((Height (cm) × Weight (kg)) / 3600)
    1. The system should not display BSA in the UI and should not allow the user to directly enter or edit BSA.
  8. The system should allow users to update Preferred Measurement System, Height, and Weight at any time while the case is in Draft state:
    1. If the Preferred Measurement System is changed after Height and Weight values have been entered, the system:
      1. should not clear the values
      2. should convert the already entered values and display them in the new format
    2. When Preferred Measurement System, Height, or Weight is updated, the system should trigger recalculation of BSA and all dependent dosing calculations without page refresh.

fields.System.Title: [BE] Create Workflow Repository
fields.System.Description:
Workflow setting will tell rule engine what steps exist in the workflow, their order, allowed transitions, define starting step, define final steps and to which workflow applies to which business process supported by NewUM (Prior authorizations (PA), appeals, etc.)
For v1, we're developing only PA workflow, but should construct underlying functionality so that it can be scaled to additional business processes with individual workflows in the future.
fields.Microsoft.VSTS.Common.AcceptanceCriteria:
1. Create necessary DB structure(s)
    1. Must support:
      1. Payer Name - name of the payer workflow applies to. 
        1. Payer name "General" will be used for general workflow that should apply for all payers for certain business process
      2. Type - type of the workflow, e.g. business process workflow applies to
        1. PA for prior authorization
        2. Appeals for appeals workflow
      3. Order - integer from 0-6, used to determine which level takes precedence during rule execution. Higher number takes precedence and overrides lower level rules. 
      4. Step Name
      5. Attribute - for specific step attributes like "starting" , "final"
      6. Allowed next step - integer defining allowed steps workflow can transition to from current step
      7. Status - workflow configuration status
      8. ValidTo - date configuration is valid to
      9. ValidFrom - date configuration is valid from
    2. Feel free to add other columns necessary for technical implementation 
  2. Create APIs to enable FE to consume and manage data
    1. Read endpoint available to everyone
    2. Create & Update endpoints available only to users in OH Admin role (role name TBC)
    3. No delete endpoint
  3. Validations:
    1. Only 1 workflow with combination Payer Name+Type can be active at any given time (check ValidTo and ValidFrom dates and status)
    2. Step names must be unique within workflow 
    3. Workflow must contain exactly one starting step
    4. Workflow must contain at least one final step
    5. No records with same order value allowed within same workflow
  4. All changes have to be audited according to NFR
  5. Create default configuration to be used when there is no payer specific configuration.
    1. Set Payer Name=General
    2. Set Type=PA
    1. Set order:

fields.System.Title: [BE] Create UI Configuration Repository
fields.System.Description:
UI configuration

should have

  • Enable custom UI configuration for specific steps (e.g. case entry or case processing) to be configured distinctly (e.g. different payers require distinct mandatory data during case intake, or custom fields that are not a part of “standard” UI)
    Overall case intake and entry will be the same across users and health plans. However, if a health plan does opt into specific programs, we may require add’l information in those scenarios.

  • UI configurations should be done on step-interface level.

Examples:

  • If a health plan doesn’t want us to review inpatient chemotherapy cases, we will require place of service entry so we can distinguish between inpatient and outpatient.

  • A health plan has added on the Site of Care program, we can require them to attest to some clinical exception

fields.System.Title: TECH
Record Financial Impact (FI) for NP → Preferred Drug Change (Case Entry)
fields.System.Description: As a CRR, CRS user I want the system to record Financial Impact data when a Non-Preferred (NP) drug is replaced with a Preferred drug during case entry, so that downstream FI reporting has the full drug and dosing context for the change.
fields.Microsoft.VSTS.Common.AcceptanceCriteria:
1. When a Preferred drug replaces an NP drug during case entry, the system should record the change for Financial Impact reporting when the case is submitted

  2. FI capture should occur when the NP → Preferred change is made via the NP preferred-selection flow and results in:

    1. replacing a drug line in an existing regimen
    2. replacing a drug line in a custom regimen
    3. switching the case to a different matching regimen that includes the selected Preferred drug
  3. FI capture should not occur for drug changes made through the Alternative Drug list control (Alternative DDL), even if the resulting selection is a Preferred drug.
  4. For FI reporting, the system should store:
    1. all dosing details for the original NP drug at the time it is replaced
    2. all dosing details for the selected Preferred drug at the time it replaces the NP drug

[BE] Populate initial dosing details based on selected regimen details
fields.System.Description: As a CRS or CRR, I want dosing fields to auto-populate based on the selected regimen and drug source so that I can start from configured defaults and complete case entry efficiently.
fields.Microsoft.VSTS.Common.AcceptanceCriteria: 1. Once the Medical and Pharmacy dosing tables are rendered for Existing Regimen, Custom Regimen, or Ancillary Drug, the system shall populate dosing table fields based on their defined source and population logic (see Data Dictionary):

fields.System.Title: [BE] Allow User Edits to Dosing with Guardrails
fields.System.Description: As a CRS or CRR, I want to edit dosing details with validation and dependency guardrails so that I can adjust treatment safely while maintaining data integrity and calculation accuracy.
fields.Microsoft.VSTS.Common.AcceptanceCriteria:
1. The system should allow users to enter or edit dosing values in the dosing tables for drugs added via Existing Regimen, Custom Regimen, and Ancillary Drug workflows (see Data Dictionary):

    2. When an editable dosing field changes, the system should recalculate dependent calculated fields once required inputs are present
    3. If required inputs for fields calculation are missing, the system should not calculate and block case progression 
    4. If a change invalidates a dependent selection (Strength - Form mismatch), the system should clear the invalid field and require a valid selection.
    5. For fields with dropdown lists, the system should restrict user selection to the available dropdown values only.
    6. When switching HCPCS within the same ingredient, the system should update HCPCS and Description only and preserve dosing values.
  2. If a defaulted Existing Regimen field is changed, the system should indicate it differs from the default:
    1. The system should retain original default and current value.

fields.System.Title: [FE] Capture Patient Height and Weight
fields.System.Description: As a CRS or CSM user, I want to enter and update patient height and weight so that dosing calculations can be performed accurately.
fields.Microsoft.VSTS.Common.AcceptanceCriteria:
1. The system should require the user to enter patient Height and Weight before allowing Main Regimen selection, Custom Regimen drug addition, or Ancillary Drug addition for Chemotherapy.
  2. The system should allow capture of the following case-level data in the UI:
    1. Preferred Measurement System
    2. Height
    3. Weight
  3. The system should provide the following Preferred Measurement System options:
    1. Standard (ft/in/lb)
    2. Standard (in/lb)
    3. Metric (cm/kg)
  4. The system should use the selected Preferred Measurement System to control the Height input format and Weight input unit as follows:
    1. Standard (ft/in/lb): Height captured as feet and inches; Weight captured in pounds
    2. Standard (in/lb): Height captured in inches; Weight captured in pounds
    3. Metric (cm/kg): Height captured in centimeters; Weight captured in kilograms
  5. The system should convert and store normalized values in calculation-ready units as follows:
    1. Height in centimeters
    2. Weight in kilograms
  6. The system should validate Height and Weight according to the rules defined in the Patient Height & Weight  and the selected Preferred Measurement System.
  7. The system should automatically calculate BSA in the background using normalized Height and Weight in accordance with the following formula: BSA = √((Height (cm) × Weight (kg)) / 3600)
    1. The system should not display BSA in the UI and should not allow the user to directly enter or edit BSA.
  8. The system should allow users to update Preferred Measurement System, Height, and Weight at any time while the case is in Draft state:
    1. If the Preferred Measurement System is changed after Height and Weight values have been entered, the system:
      1. should not clear the values
      2. should convert the already entered values and display them in the new format
    2. When Preferred Measurement System, Height, or Weight is updated, the system should trigger recalculation of BSA and all dependent dosing calculations without page refresh.

fields.System.Title: Design System: Tabs
fields.System.Description:
Component name:
Tabs

Link to handover file:
https://www.figma.com/design/fLTO0zsOOSkF2oODKE0KVk/OH-NF-UI-Kit?node-id=739-800

Context:
Used for navigation between related content sections within the same view. Helps organize information without page reload. Common in dashboards, settings, and content grouping.

States and variants covered:
Default, Hover, Active (Selected), Focus, Disabled

States:

  • Default — inactive tab

  • Hover — pointer over tab

  • Active (Selected) — current tab

  • Focus — keyboard navigation state

  • Disabled — unavailable tab

Sizes:

  • S — compact (tight layouts)

  • M — default

  • L — prominent (page-level navigation)

With/without label:

  • Label only (text)

  • Label + icon

  • Icon only (rare, for dense UI)

Label position:

  • Horizontal (left to right)

  • Centered (for equal-width tabs)
fields.Microsoft.VSTS.Common.AcceptanceCriteria:
• Indicator aligns correctly with tab content

  • Supports dynamic number of tabs

  • Tokens applied consistently across variants

  • Works across sizes without layout shifts

fields.System.Title: Design System: Tables
fields.System.Description:
Component name:
Table

Link to handover file:
https://www.figma.com/design/fLTO0zsOOSkF2oODKE0KVk/OH-NF-UI-Kit?node-id=841-1459

Context:
Used to display structured data in rows and columns for comparison, analysis, and quick scanning. Supports large datasets, sorting, and inline actions. Designed for dashboards, admin panels, and data-heavy interfaces.

States and variants covered:
Default, Hover, Active, Selected, Focus, Disabled, Loading, Empty

States:

  • Default — standard data view

  • Hover — row highlight on mouse over

  • Active — interaction state (click/press)

  • Selected — row or cell selected

  • Focus — keyboard navigation state

  • Disabled — non-interactive row/cell

  • Loading — skeleton or spinner

  • Empty — no data available

Sizes:

  • S — compact (dense data view)

  • M — default

  • L — spacious (readability-focused)


Layout:

  • Fixed columns

  • Flexible (auto width)

  • Scrollable (horizontal/vertical)

  • With pagination

  • With expandable rows

  • With selectable rows (checkbox/radio)


Token dependencies:

  • Color: background, border, hover, selected, text-primary, text-secondary

  • Typography: header, cell text

  • Spacing: row height, cell padding

  • Border radius: table container

  • Elevation: sticky header / overlays

  • Interaction tokens: hover/active/focus states
fields.Microsoft.VSTS.Common.AcceptanceCriteria:
• All states are visually distinct and accessible (WCAG compliant)

  • Supports keyboard navigation (tab, arrows, selection)

  • Responsive behavior defined (scroll, collapse, or stack)

  • Tokens are consistently applied across all variants

  • Works with dynamic data (0 → large datasets)

  • Interaction states (hover, selected, focus) behave consistently

  • Header alignment matches column content

  • No visual breaks in edge cases (long text, empty cells, overflow)

fields.Microsoft.VSTS.Common.AcceptanceCriteria:
1. When the user adds an ancillary drug, the system should check the drug’s DiagnosisGroupId against ancillary drugs already associated to the case.

fields.System.Title: Create Case domain around Case schema
fields.System.Description:
This module is one of the trickiest to write. Much of the system will depend on its correctness, and it will eventually need to manage:
  • write-locked, denormalized case schema (potentially stored as SQLite blobs)
  • Case Meta Schema in OLTP DB
  • case data in the Reporting OLAP DB (later)
Case data may come from:
  • the UI via the API gateway
  • bulk ingest from migration pipeline
  • rules inference
Each of these sources corresponds to separately-deployed code that could call this module. Because each case SQLite will be write-locked, there may be contention. This module will need to coexist with instances of itself running on different machines. 

When eligibility records update, the Member Admin module will need to call this module in order to add new eligibility records to related open cases. 

Not all of these problems have to be solved now, but the interface should be sufficient to hide the solutions. 

See the Data subsystem of this draft architecture diagram.
fields.Microsoft.VSTS.Common.AcceptanceCriteria:
• DAL can be configured to read/write SQLite files from blob storage or the local filesystem, similar to how OLTP can either be local or remote
  • unit tests for operations listed in description
  • underlying schema is treated as write-only

fields.System.Title: Enforce Product Line Intake Scope Rule
fields.System.Description: As a Case Review Specialist, I want the system to evaluate whether a member’s product line is allowed for intake when a case is submitted, so that cases associated with unsupported product lines are created and then closed as out of scope before review begins
fields.Microsoft.VSTS.Common.AcceptanceCriteria:
Preconditions

  • An Intake Coordinator has searched for a member and the member was found, in accordance with https://oncologyanalytics.visualstudio.com/newUM/_workitems/edit/179603/?view=edit
  • A draft case exists for the member.
  • Patient demographic information and eligibility data for the member is available from the eligibility file or can be retrieved, in accordance with https://oncologyanalytics.visualstudio.com/newUM/_workitems/edit/179607/?view=edit
  • Eligibility verification has completed successfully and the member is determined to be eligible, in accordance with
  • https://oncologyanalytics.visualstudio.com/newUM/_workitems/edit/179611/?view=edit
  • The Intake Coordinator initiates Submit Case.


Acceptance Criteria
  • The rule is evaluated as part of Submit Case processing.
  • The rule applies only when the member is determined to be eligible.
  • The member’s product line (Line of Business) from eligibility data is compared against the payer’s configured intake scope.
    • If the member’s product line is not enabled in payer configuration:
      • The case can be submitted.

      • Upon submission, t

        he case status is updated to Out of Scope.
      • The case is administratively closed in accordance with intake scope rules.
    • If the member’s product line is enabled in payer configuration:
      • The case can be submitted.

      • Upon submission, t

        he case status is updated to Ready for Assignment.

Postconditions

  • When the product line is out of scope:

    • A case record exists containing:

      • Patient demographic information
      • Eligibility information 
    • The case is in a closed Out of Scope state.
    • The closure reason reflects a product line intake scope restriction.

    • The following are recorded and available for audit:

      • Rule evaluation outcome
      • Case status transition
      • Closure reason

[BE] Search for a member using required inputs (guardrails)
fields.System.Description: As a Provider Office Staff, CRS, CRR, I want to search for a member using strict, validated search field combinations, so that I can identify the correct member to start case entry, while only seeing members for the health plan(s) I’m authorized to access.
fields.Microsoft.VSTS.Common.AcceptanceCriteria:
1. The system must provide a Member Search capability prior to new case creation
  2. The system must uniquely identify exactly one member entity prior to allowing progression in Case Entry.
    1. For R1, all authenticated users have access to all configured Health Plans.

  3. Search execution must require one of the following combinations:

    1. Health Plan + Date of Birth + Member ID
    2. Health Plan + Date of Birth + First Name + Last Name
    3. Health Plan + Date of Birth + Member ID + First Name + Last Name
  4. The system must validate all search fields in accordance with the Data Dictionary and block execution if validation fails.
  5. The system must enforce payer-specific configuration for Member ID for the selected Health Plan, including:
    1. Max Member ID Length (Required in Payer Configs)
    2. Member ID Expression / Pattern (Optional in Payer Configs)
    3. Member ID Message  (Optional in Payer Configs)
  6. The system must execute the search strictly within the selected Health Plan 
  7. Matching must be exact; when multiple identifiers are provided, all identifiers must match the same member entity.
  8. The system must return Found when exactly one distinct member entity matches the criteria.
  9. The system must return Not Found when no member matches the criteria
    1. Edge cases related to Member Search
  10. The system must log each search attempt

Technical Considerations:
  1. The eligibility ingestion and ETL pipeline are out of scope for NF Data team. Member Search consumes eligibility data as provided by the OH data team.
  2. Search must return a single logical member entity
  3. No identity reconciliation or record merging is performed in R1.
  4. Configs (from OADB) are out of scope for this story > stub for fetching the configs

fields.System.Title: [BE] View/Edit Member Information Form for Found Member (core eligibility data + optional eligibility attributes )
fields.System.Description:
As a Provider Office Staff, CRS, CRR, I want the system to retrieve and present member information after a member is found,
So that I can review demographic, contact, eligibility, and basic treatment context before proceeding with case entry decisions.

Story Scope / Boundaries:
  • Triggered when Member Search returns Found (single logical member)
  • Retrieves member -related data associated with the identified member
  • Makes member information available for review and limited modification where permitted
  • Does not create a draft case
  • Does not perform eligibility-at-time-of-service validation or scope determination (handled by subsequent stories)
fields.Microsoft.VSTS.Common.AcceptanceCriteria:
1. The system must render the Member Information form after a successful Member Search result (Found).
  2. The form must display member information organized into logical groups as defined in the Data Dictionary:
    1. Demographic information
    2. Member  contact information
    3. Member representative information (when available)
    4. Eligibility information - all required fields
    5. Basic treatment information (limited to treatment start date)
  3. All eligibility-derived member information must be displayed as read-only.
  4. The system must not allow editing, correction, or override of eligibility-derived data in R1.
  5. Treatment Start Date must be displayed as empty by default:
    1. The system must allow the user to enter or modify Treatment Start Date.
  6. Displaying the Member Information form must not create, persist, or update a case record.
  7. If required eligibility attributes (as defined in the Data Dictionary) are missing, the system must prevent progression to downstream steps and display a blocking message - see Edge Cases for Display Member Information
  8. If optional eligibility attributes (as defined in the Data Dictionary) are missing, then:

    1. op

      tional attributes are not displayed or displayed blank per UI design
    2. do not block progression

fields.System.Title: Display Member Information Form When Multiple Matches Are Returned
fields.System.Description: As a Provider Office Staff, CRS, CRR, I want the system to present member information even when multiple eligibility records exist, so that I can proceed with eligibility verification at the appropriate step.
fields.Microsoft.VSTS.Common.AcceptanceCriteria:
1. When Member Search identifies a member, the system displays the Member Information form once, even if multiple eligibility records exist for that member.
  2. The system represents the member as a single member entity, with eligibility information reflecting one or more associated eligibility records.
  3. The system does not require the user to select a specific eligibility record at this stage.
  4. Eligibility details are displayed to provide visibility only; no eligibility prioritization or selection occurs at this step.
  5. The user may proceed to enter the Treatment Start Date and trigger eligibility verification.

Considerations

  •  UI/UX for displaying multiple eligibility records
  • Preferred model: one member with multiple eligibility records behind it.
    • Continuous coverage periods should be treated as a single coverage span where possible; non-continuous periods remain separate.
    • If a Health Plan–provided member identifier is not available (legacy plans), the system may not be able to reliably group eligibility records; behavior in such cases must be defined (TBD).
  • No eligibility filtering or resolution is performed in this story.

fields.System.Title: [BE] Validate Member Eligibility at Treatment Start Date
fields.System.Description: As a Provider Office Staff, CRS, CRR, I want the system to validate that the member has active coverage on the treatment start date, so that only eligible cases can proceed.
fields.Microsoft.VSTS.Common.AcceptanceCriteria:
1. Eligibility verification may be initiated only after:
    1. A member has been successfully identified, and
    2. Patient Information has been saved with a Treatment Start Date.
  2. Treatment Start Date is the authoritative reference date for eligibility verification.

  3. Upon initiation, the system must evaluate whether the member has coverage active on the Treatment Start Date using: 

    Coverage Effective Date, Coverage Expiration Date
  4. If multiple eligibility records exist for the member under the selected Health Plan, the system must evaluate all returned eligibility records.

  5. An eligibility record is considered active if:

    1. Treatment Start Date is greater than or equal to Coverage Effective Date, and
    2. Treatment Start Date is less than or equal to Coverage Expiration Date
  6. If at least one eligibility record is active on the Treatment Start Date, the system must return outcome: Eligible.
  7. If no eligibility record is active on the Treatment Start Date, the system must return outcome: Not Eligible.

  8. If the outcome is Not Eligible, the system must:

    1. Prevent progression to scope determination, and
    2. Block further progression in the case entry flow
    3. Edge Cases – Eligibility Verification
  9. The system records the eligibility verification outcome and the eligibility record(s) evaluated for audit.

fields.System.Title: Determine Member Scope (In-Scope vs Carve-Out)
fields.System.Description: As a Provider Office Staff, CRS, CRR, I want the system to determine whether an eligible member request is in scope for OncoHealth review, so that only appropriate cases proceed to case creation and review.
fields.Microsoft.VSTS.Common.AcceptanceCriteria:
1. The system determines whether the eligible member/request is In Scope or Out of Scope (Carve-Out) for OncoHealth review.
  2. Scope evaluation uses eligibility attributes received from the Health Plan.

  3. The system evaluates the following eligibility fields:

    1. Delegation indicator
    2. Employer Group ID
    3. Line of Business
    4. Coverage Product / Plan Code
  4. If any eligibility field indicates non-delegation or carve-out, the member is classified as Out of Scope.
  5. If no carve-out condition is met, the member is classified as In Scope.
  6. Scope evaluation is Health Plan–specific and uses payer configuration defined in Admin Portal.
  7. Scope determination occurs before case submission and before clinical workflows.
    1. If In Scope, the user may proceed to draft case creation.
    1. If Out of Scope, the system blocks progression to standard case creation and returns an Out-of-Scope outcome.

Considerations:
  1. Define scope determination rules and coverage attributes, such as: Line of Business, Product Line / Product Offering, Employer Group / Benefit Package, State of Plan Issue

fields.System.Title: [BE] Create Draft Case and Save Progress
fields.System.Description: As a Provider Office Staff, CRS, CRR, I want to create a draft case and save my progress during case entry, so that I can either pause and resume later or continue directly with diagnosis and treatment selection.
fields.Microsoft.VSTS.Common.AcceptanceCriteria:
1. The system must allow Draft Case creation once a member has been successfully identified and the Member Information form is rendered in accordance with:
    1. Search for a member using required inputs (guardrails)
    2. View/Edit Member Information Form for Found Member (core eligibility data): 
      1. Treatment Start Date must be displayed as empty by default:
        1. The system must allow the user to enter or modify Treatment Start Date.

        2. Draft Case creation must not require: 

          Eligibility verification and Scope determination

  2. The system must provide two actions:

    1. Save as Draft and remain on the current step.
    2. Save as Draft and proceed to the next step:
      1. Eligibility verification is required

  3. Upon Draft Case creation, the system must:

    1. Generate and assign a unique Case Identifier.
    2. Persist all data captured to date.
    3. Set Case Status to Draft.
    4. Record Created Date/Time and Created By.
  4. The system must allow saving the Draft throughout case entry.
  5. The user must be able to retrieve and resume the Draft Case at any time.
  6. All Draft Case creation and updates must be logged for audit.
Considerations:
  1. Case State Model > Draft:
    1. Created after member selection.
    2. May contain incomplete data.
    3. Eligibility and scope may be pending or not evaluated.
    4. User may edit and save repeatedly.

The goal is to validate FE -BE contract alignment, data persistence, and basic happy-path behavior, not to implement full clinical rules or formulary logic.

Scope Covered (by integration)

  • Capture Diagnosis and Clinical Indication

  • Select Assignment Type (Chemo only)
  • Enter Chemo Protocol Details
  • Display Submission Confirmation

fields.System.Title: Testing UX User Story
fields.System.Description:
Link to wireframes:
https://figma.com/file/abc456/UX-Wireframes-Q1?node-id=2345:6789

User story:
As a customer completing a purchase, I need to select my preferred payment method so that I can pay for my order securely and conveniently.

Flow context:
This is step 2 of 3 in the checkout flow:

  1. Shipping address
  2. Payment method (this screen)
  3. Order review

User arrives here after completing shipping address. After selecting payment and entering details, they proceed to order review.

Key interactions:

  • User can choose between saved payment methods or add a new one
  • Saved cards show last 4 digits and card brand icon
  • "Add new payment method" expands inline form below saved methods
  • CVV required for saved cards (security best practice)
  • Default payment method is pre-selected
  • "Continue to review" button disabled until valid payment selected

Open questions:

  • Should we support PayPal/Apple Pay on this screen or handle those separately?
  • Do we show billing address on this screen or assume it matches shipping?
  • Should users be able to edit saved payment methods here or only in account settings?
fields.Microsoft.VSTS.Common.AcceptanceCriteria:
• High-fidelity design matches wireframe structure and user flow
  • All interactive states defined (hover, focus, disabled, error)
  • Empty state designed (no saved payment methods)
  • Error states designed (invalid card, declined card, expired card)
  • Design system components used where available
  • Accessibility annotations included
  • Developer handoff documentation complete

fields.System.Title: [BE] Load ICD-10 Library with Diagnosis Group
fields.System.Description: As a CRS, Provider Staff, or Health Plan User, I want the system to load ICD-10 diagnoses from the Admin Portal, so that I can select a valid diagnosis that is consistent with centralized clinical configuration.
fields.Microsoft.VSTS.Common.AcceptanceCriteria:
1. The system shall retrieve ICD-10 records from NewUM reference data populated via the migration pipeline.

  2. For each ICD-10 record, the system shall load:

    1. ICD-10 Code
    2. ICD-10 Description
    3. Diagnosis Group
    4. Treatment Type Applicability (e.g., Chemo, GMT, XRT) 
    5. Payer Applicability (All or specific Payers)
    6. Active Status: The system shall return only ICD-10 records where Active Status = true.

  3. Diagnosis Group shall be system-derived based on ICD-10 mapping and shall not be editable in NewUM.
  4. The system shall persist the ICD reference data version identifier and load timestamp at case creation.
  5. The system shall record the reference data version or timestamp used for diagnosis retrieval for audit purposes.

  6. If ICD reference data is unavailable:

    1. Diagnosis selection shall be blocked.
    2. A recoverable system error shall be displayed.
    3. The failure shall be logged.
Technical Considerations
  • Data contract must guarantee non-null Diagnosis Group for each ICD-10.
  • Check whether ICD-10 records contain: Active Status flag, Effective Start / End Dates
  • TBD: Spike to investigate data source: Drugs API and Preferred Regimens:
    • Dx group
    • Treatment Type Applicability (e.g., Chemo, GMT, XRT)
    • Payer Applicability (All or specific Payers)

fields.System.Title: [FE] Select ICD-10 code for Dx
fields.System.Description: As a CRS , I want to search and select an ICD-10 diagnosis, So that the case contains the required diagnosis for prior authorization.
fields.Microsoft.VSTS.Common.AcceptanceCriteria:
1. The system should allow the user to search ICD-10 codes by:
    1. ICD-10 code (exact or partial)
    2. ICD-10 description (keyword)
    3. Data Dictionary
  2. The system shall return only ICD-10 records that are mapped to the active Diagnosis Group context
  3. The system shall display, for each ICD-10 search result:
    1. ICD-10 code
    2. ICD-10 description
  4. The system shall allow the user to select exactly one ICD-10 from the search results.
  5. The system shall prevent progression to the next workflow step until an ICD-10 is selected.
  6. Upon ICD-10 selection, the system shall persist on the case: ICD-10 code, ICD-10 description, Diagnosis Group identifier and name
  7. If the selected ICD-10 does not have a valid Diagnosis Group mapping, the system shall:
    1. Block selection
    2. Display an error message
  8. If the user changes the selected ICD-10, the system shall clear dependent diagnosis attributes: 
    1. Cancer Stage

Technical Considerations
  • Diagnosis Group is system-derived from the ICD-10 mapping and is not user-editable.
  • Diagnosis Group (not the raw ICD-10 code) is used as the primary input for regimen search downstream.
  • Treatment Type filtering and payer-specific diagnosis restrictions are enforced by separate stories and consume applicability attributes loaded via reference data.

fields.System.Title: [BE] Select Cancer Stage for Diagnosis
fields.System.Description: As a CRS, Provider Staff, or Health Plan User, I want to select a cancer stage relevant to the chosen diagnosis, So that reviewers have appropriate clinical context during evaluation.
fields.Microsoft.VSTS.Common.AcceptanceCriteria:
1. The system should require an ICD-10 diagnosis to be selected before a Cancer Stage value can be entered:
    1. Data Dictionary
  2. The system should limit Cancer Stage options to values relevant to the selected diagnosis (Diagnosis Group).
  3. The system should require Cancer Stage selection before the user can proceed to the next workflow step.
  4. The system should allow selection of exactly one Cancer Stage value.
  5. The system should save the selected Cancer Stage on the case.
  6. If the user changes the selected ICD-10 diagnosis, the system should clear the previously selected Cancer Stage.
  7. After ICD-10 change, the system should require Cancer Stage to be selected again before proceeding.

fields.System.Title: Prototype interface between rules engine and case queuing system
fields.Microsoft.VSTS.Common.AcceptanceCriteria:
We need to know how the rules engine will modify case state in a way that:
  • helps the queuing system route cases (what role needs to look at this case next?)
  • can tweak priority (TaT is almost up, someone must resolve this case!)
The rules engine should not actually control the queuing; that functionality is too strongly coupled to UI/UX decisions.
